CopyHeaderChoice;\n32: \n33: /*\n34:  * Represents where to save input processing errors.  More values to be added\n35:  * in the future.\n36:  */\n37: typedef enum CopyOnErrorChoice\n38: {\n39: \tCOPY_ON_ERROR_STOP = 0,\t\t/* immediately throw errors, default */\n40: \tCOPY_ON_ERROR_IGNORE,\t\t/* ignore errors */\n41: } CopyOnErrorChoice;\n42: \n43: /*\n44:  * Represents verbosity of logged messages by COPY command.\n45:  */\n46: typedef enum CopyLogVerbosityChoice\n47: {\n48: \tCOPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_SILENT = -1, /* logs none */\n49: \tCOPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_DEFAULT = 0, /* logs no additional messages. As this is\n50: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t * the default, assign 0 */\n51: \tCOPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_VERBOSE, /* logs additional messages */\n52: } CopyLogVerbosityChoice;\n53: \n54: /*\n55:  * A struct to hold COPY options, in a parsed form. *delim;\t\t\t/* column delimiter (must be 1 byte) */\n74: \tchar\t   *quote;\t\t\t/* CSV quote char (must be 1 byte) */\n75: \tchar\t   *escape;\t\t\t/* CSV escape char (must be 1 byte) */\n76: In the \\\"TO\\\" case\\n47:  * we also support copying the output of an arbitrary SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE\\n48:  * or DELETE query.\\n49:  *\\n50:  * If <pipe> is false, transfer is between the table and the file named\\n51:  * <filename>.  Otherwise, transfer is between the table and our regular\\n52:  * input/output stream. The latter could be either stdin/stdout or a\\n53:  * socket, depending on whether we're running under Postmaster control.\\n54:  *\\n55:  * Do not allow a Postgres user without the 'pg_read_server_files' or\\n56:  * 'pg_write_server_files' role to read from or write to a file.\\n57:  *\\n58:  * Do not allow the copy if user doesn't have proper permission to access\\n59:  * the table or the specifically requested columns.\\n60:  */\\n61: void\\n62: DoCopy(ParseState *pstate, const CopyStmt *stmt,\\n63: \\t   int stmt_location, int stmt_len,\\n64: \\t   uint64 *processed)\\n65: {\\n66: \\tbool\\t\\tis_from = stmt->is_from;\\n67: \\tbool\\t\\tpipe = (stmt->filename == NULL);\\n68: \\tRelation\\trel;\\n69: \\tOid\\t\\t\\trelid;\\n70: \\tRawStmt    *query = NULL;\\n71: \\tNode\\t   *whereClause = NULL;\\n72: \\n73: \\t/*\\n74: \\t * Disallow COPY to/from file or program except to users with the\\n75: \\t * appropriate role.\\n76: \\t */\\n77: \\tif (!pipe)\\n78: \\t{\\n79: \\t\\tif (stmt->is_program)\\n80: \\t\\t{\\n81: \\t\\t\\tif (!has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_EXECUTE_SERVER_PROGRAM))\\n82: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n83: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\\n84: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"permission denied to COPY to or from an external program\\\"),\\n85: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" role may COPY to or from an external program.\\\",\\n86: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   \\\"pg_execute_server_program\\\"),\\n87: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \\\"\\n88: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t \\\"psql's \\\\\\\\copy command also works for anyone.\\\")));\\n89: \\t\\t}\\n90: \\t\\telse\\n91: \\t\\t{\\n92: \\t\\t\\tif (is_from && !has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_READ_SERVER_FILES))\\n93: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n94: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"permission denied to COPY from a file\\\"),\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with file\\\"),\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" role may COPY to a file.\\\",\\n106: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t RowExclusiveLock : AccessShareLock;\\n115: \\t\\tParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;\\n116: \\t\\tRTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;\\n117: \\t\\tTupleDesc\\ttupDesc;\\n118: \\t\\tList\\t   *attnums;\\n119: \\t\\tListCell   *cur;\\n120: \\n121: \\t\\tAssert(!stmt->query);\\n122: \\n123: \\t\\t/* Open and lock the relation, using the appropriate lock type. */\\n124: \\t\\trel = table_openrv(stmt->relation, lockmode);\\n125: \\n126: \\t\\trelid = RelationGetRelid(rel);\\n127: \\n128: \\t\\tnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel, lockmode,\\n129: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   NULL, false, false);\\n130: \\n131: \\t\\tperminfo = nsitem->p_perminfo;\\n132: \\t\\tperminfo->requiredPerms = (is_from ? ACL_INSERT : ACL_SELECT);\\n133: \\n134: \\t\\tif (stmt->whereClause)\\n135: \\t\\t{\\n136: \\t\\t\\tBitmapset  *expr_attrs = NULL;\\n137: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\ti;\\n138: \\n139: \\t\\t\\t/* add nsitem to query namespace */\\n140: \\t\\t\\taddNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);\\n141: \\n142: \\t\\t\\t/* Transform the raw expression tree */\\n143: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = transformExpr(pstate, stmt->whereClause, EXPR_KIND_COPY_WHERE);\\n144: \\n145: \\t\\t\\t/* Make sure it yields a boolean result. */\\n146: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = coerce_to_boolean(pstate, whereClause, \\\"WHERE\\\");\\n147: \\n148: \\t\\t\\t/* we have to fix its collations too */\\n149: \\t\\t\\tassign_expr_collations(pstate, whereClause);\\n150: \\n151: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n152: \\t\\t\\t * Examine all the columns in the WHERE clause expression.  When\\n153: \\t\\t\\t * the whole-row reference is present, examine all the columns of\\n154: \\t\\t\\t * the table.\\n155: \\t\\t\\t */\\n156: \\t\\t\\tpull_varattnos(whereClause, 1, &expr_attrs);\\n157: \\t\\t\\tif (bms_is_member(0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber, expr_attrs))\\n158: \\t\\t\\t{\\n159: \\t\\t\\t\\texpr_attrs = bms_add_range(expr_attrs,\\n160: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   1 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,\\n161: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\\n162: \\t\\t\\t\\texpr_attrs = bms_del_member(expr_attrs, 0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\\n163: \\t\\t\\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t\\ti = -1;\\n166: \\t\\t\\twhile ((i = bms_next_member(expr_attrs, i)) >= 0)\\n167: \\t\\t\\t{\\n168: \\t\\t\\t\\tAttrNumber\\tattno = i + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\\n169: \\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\tAssert(attno != 0);\\n171: \\n172: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n173: \\t\\t\\t\\t * Prohibit generated columns in the WHERE clause.  Stored\\n174: \\t\\t\\t\\t * generated columns are not yet computed when the filtering\\n175: \\t\\t\\t\\t * happens.  Virtual generated columns could probably work (we\\n176: \\t\\t\\t\\t * would need to expand them somewhere around here), but for\\n177: \\t\\t\\t\\t * now we keep them consistent with the stored variant.\\n178: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n179: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (TupleDescAttr(RelationGetDescr(rel), attno - 1)->attgenerated)\\n180: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n181: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),\\n182: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrmsg(\\\"generated columns are not supported in COPY FROM WHERE conditions\\\"),\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrdetail(\\\"Column \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" is a generated column.\\\",\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t  get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel), attno, false)));\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\n187: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = eval_const_expressions(NULL, whereClause);\\n188: \\n189: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = (Node *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) whereClause, false);\\n190: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) whereClause);\\n191: \\t\\t}\\n192: \\n193: \\t\\ttupDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);\\n194: \\t\\tattnums = CopyGetAttnums(tupDesc, rel, stmt->attlist);\\n195: \\t\\tforeach(cur, attnums)\\n196: \\t\\t{\\n197: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tattno;\\n198: \\t\\t\\tBitmapset **bms;\\n199: \\n200: \\t\\t\\tattno = lfirst_int(cur) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\\n201: \\t\\t\\tbms = is_from ? &perminfo->insertedCols : &perminfo->selectedCols;\\n202: \\n203: \\t\\t\\t*bms = bms_add_member(*bms, attno);\\n204: \\t\\t}\\n205: \\t\\tExecCheckPermissions(pstate->p_rtable, list_make1(perminfo), true);\\n206: \\n207: \\t\\t/*\\n208: \\t\\t * Permission check for row security policies.\\n209: \\t\\t *\\n210: \\t\\t * check_enable_rls will ereport(ERROR) if the user has requested\\n211: \\t\\t * something invalid and will otherwise indicate if we should enable\\n212: \\t\\t * RLS (returns RLS_ENABLED) or not for this COPY statement.\\n213: \\t\\t *\\n214: \\t\\t * If the relation has a row security policy and we are to apply it\\n215: \\t\\t * then perform a \\\"query\\\" copy and allow the normal query processing\\n216: \\t\\t * to handle the policies.\\n217: \\t\\t *\\n218: \\t\\t * If RLS is not enabled for this, then just fall through to the\\n219: \\t\\t * normal non-filtering relation handling.\\n220: \\t\\t */\\n221: \\t\\tif (check_enable_rls(relid, InvalidOid, false) == RLS_ENABLED)\\n222: \\t\\t{\\n223: \\t\\t\\tSelectStmt *select;\\n224: \\t\\t\\tColumnRef  *cr;\\n225: \\t\\t\\tResTarget  *target;\\n226: \\t\\t\\tRangeVar   *from;\\n227: \\t\\t\\tList\\t   *targetList = NIL;\\n228: \\n229: \\t\\t\\tif (is_from)\\n230: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n231: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY FROM not supported with row-level security\\\"),\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Use INSERT statements instead.\\\")));\\n234: \\n235: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n236: \\t\\t\\t * Build target list\\n237: \\t\\t\\t *\\n238: \\t\\t\\t * If no columns are specified in the attribute list of the COPY\\n239: \\t\\t\\t * command, then the target list is 'all' columns. Therefore, '*'\\n240: \\t\\t\\t * should be used as the target list for the resulting SELECT\\n241: \\t\\t\\t * statement.\\n242: \\t\\t\\t *\\n243: \\t\\t\\t * In the case that columns are specified in the attribute list,\\n244: \\t\\t\\t * create a ColumnRef and ResTarget for each column and add them\\n245: \\t\\t\\t * to the target list for the resulting SELECT statement.\\n246: \\t\\t\\t */\\n247: \\t\\t\\tif (!stmt->attlist)\\n248: \\t\\t\\t{\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr->location = -1;\\n252: \\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\\n254: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->name = NULL;\\n255: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\\n256: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\\n257: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->location = -1;\\n258: \\n259: \\t\\t\\t\\ttargetList = list_make1(target);\\n260: \\t\\t\\t}\\n261: \\t\\t\\telse\\n262: \\t\\t\\t{\\n263: \\t\\t\\t\\tListCell   *lc;\\n264: \\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\tforeach(lc, stmt->attlist)\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * Build the ColumnRef for each column.  The ColumnRef\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * 'fields' property is a String node that corresponds to\\n270: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * the column name respectively.\\n271: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n272: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\\n273: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr->fields = list_make1(lfirst(lc));\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr->location = -1;\\n275: \\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* Build the ResTarget and add the ColumnRef to it. */\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\\n278: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->name = NULL;\\n279: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\\n280: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\\n281: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->location = -1;\\n282: \\n283: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* Add each column to the SELECT statement's target list */\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttargetList = lappend(targetList, target);\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n286: \\t\\t\\t}\\n287: \\n288: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n289: \\t\\t\\t * Build RangeVar for from clause, fully qualified based on the\\n290: \\t\\t\\t * relation which we have opened and locked. */\\n294: \\t\\t\\tfrom = makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(rel)),\\n295: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(rel)),\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t-1);\\n297: \\t\\t\\tfrom->inh = false;\\t/* apply ONLY */\\n298: \\n299: \\t\\t\\t/* Build query */\\n300: \\t\\t\\tselect = makeNode(SelectStmt);\\n301: \\t\\t\\tselect->targetList = targetList;\\n302: \\t\\t\\tselect->fromClause = list_make1(from);\\n303: \\n304: \\t\\t\\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\\n305: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt = (Node *) select;\\n306: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt_location = stmt_location;\\n307: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt_len = stmt_len;\\n308: \\n309: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n310: \\t\\t\\t * Close the relation for now, but keep the lock on it to prevent\\n311: \\t\\t\\t * changes between now and when we start the query-based COPY.\\n312: \\t\\t\\t *\\n313: \\t\\t\\t * We'll reopen it later as part of the query-based COPY.\\n314: \\t\\t\\t */\\n315: \\t\\t\\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\\n316: \\t\\t\\trel = NULL;\\n317: \\t\\t}\\n318: \\t}\\n319: \\telse\\n320: \\t{\\n321: \\t\\tAssert(stmt->query);\\n322: \\n323: \\t\\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\\n324: \\t\\tquery->stmt = \\tif (is_from)\\n333: \\t{\\n334: \\t\\tCopyFromState cstate;\\n335: \\n336: \\t\\tAssert(rel);\\n337: \\n338: \\t\\t/* check read-only transaction and parallel mode */\\n339: \\t\\tif (XactReadOnly \\t\\tEndCopyFrom(cstate);\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\telse\\n349: \\t{\\n350: \\t\\tCopyToState cstate;\\n351: \\n352: \\t\\tcstate = BeginCopyTo(pstate, rel, query, relid,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t \\t\\tEndCopyTo(cstate);\\n357: \\t}\\n358: \\n359: \\tif (rel != NULL)\\n360: \\t\\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: /*\\n364:  * Extract a CopyHeaderChoice value from a DefElem.  This is like\\n365:  * defGetBoolean() but also accepts the special value \\\"match\\\".\\n366:  */\\n367: static CopyHeaderChoice\\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\\n369: {\\n370: \\t/*\\n371: \\t * If no parameter value given, assume \\\"true\\\" is meant.\\n372: \\t */\\n373: \\tif (def->arg == NULL)\\n374: \\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n375: \\n376: \\t/*\\n377: \\t * Allow 0, 1, \\\"true\\\", \\\"false\\\", \\\"on\\\", \\\"off\\\", or \\\"match\\\".\\n378: \\t */\\n379: \\tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\\n380: \\t{\\n381: \\t\\tcase T_Integer:\\n382: \\t\\t\\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\\n383: \\t\\t\\t{\\n384: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n385: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n401: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"true\\\") == 0)\\n402: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n403: \\t\\t\\t\\tif /*\\n461:  * Extract REJECT_LIMIT value from a DefElem.\\n462:  *\\n463:  * REJECT_LIMIT can be specified in two ways: as an int64 for the COPY command\\n464:  * option or as a single-quoted string COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_DEFAULT;\\t/* keep compiler quiet */\\n516: }\\n517: \\n518: /*\\n519:  * Process the statement option list for COPY.\\n520:  *\\n521:  * Scan the options list (a list of DefElem) *\\n526:  * This is exported so that external users of the COPY API can sanity-check\\n527:  * a list of options.  In that usage, 'opts_out' can be passed as NULL and\\n528:  * the collected data is just leaked until CurrentMemoryContext is reset.\\n529:  *\\n530:  * Note that additional checking, such as whether column names listed in FORCE\\n531:  * QUOTE actually exist, has to be applied later. (opts_out->default_print)\\n920: \\t{\\n921: \\t\\tif (!is_from)\\n922: \\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n923: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n924: \\t\\t\\t/*- translator: first %s \\\"DEFAULT\\\",\\n927: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"COPY TO\\\")));\\n928: \\n929: \\t\\t/* Don't allow the delimiter to appear in the default string. */\\n930: \\t\\tif (strchr(opts_out->default_print, */\\n934: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY delimiter character must not appear in the %s specification\\\",\\n935: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"DEFAULT\\\")));\\n936: \\n937: \\t\\t/* Don't allow the CSV \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n942: \\t\\t\\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. NULL */\\n943: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"CSV quote character must not \\t}\\n954: \\t/* Check on_error */\\n955: \\tif (opts_out->binary && opts_out->on_error != COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP)\\n956: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n957: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\\n958: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\\n963: \\t\\t/*- translator: first and second %s are the names of COPY option, e.g.\\n964: \\t\\t * ON_ERROR, third is the value of the COPY option, e.g. IGNORE */\\n965: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY %s requires %s to be set to %s\\\",\\n966: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"REJECT_LIMIT\\\", \\\"ON_ERROR\\\", \\\"IGNORE\\\")));\\n967: }\\n968: \\n969: /*\\n970:  * CopyGetAttnums - build an integer list of attnums to be copied\\n971:  *\\n972:  * The input attnamelist is either the user-specified column list,\\n973:  * or NIL if there was none (in which case we want all the non-dropped\\n974:  * columns).\\n975:  *\\n976:  * We don't include generated columns in the generated full list and we don't\\n977:  * allow them to be specified explicitly.  They don't make sense for COPY\\n978:  * FROM, but we could possibly allow them for COPY TO.  But this way it's at\\n979:  * least ensured that whatever we copy out can be copied back in.\\n980:  *\\n981:  * rel can be NULL ... it's only used for error reports.\\n982:  */\\n983: List *\\n984: CopyGetAttnums(TupleDesc tupDesc, Relation rel, List *attnamelist)\\n985: {\\n986: \\tList\\t   *attnums = NIL;\\n987: \\n988: \\tif (attnamelist == NIL)\\n989: \\t{\\n990: \\t\\t/* Generate default 1);\\n1001: \\t\\t}\\n1002: \\t}\\n1003: \\telse\\n1004: \\t{\\n1005: \\t\\t/* Validate the user-supplied list and extract attnums */\\n1006: \\t\\tListCell   *l;\\n1007: \\n1008: \\t\\tforeach(l, attnamelist)\\n1009: \\t\\t{\\n1010: \\t\\t\\tchar\\t   *name = strVal(lfirst(l));\\n1011: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tattnum;\\n1012: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\ti;\\n1013: \\n1014: \\t\\t\\t/* Lookup column name \\n1020: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (att->attisdropped)\\n1021: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue;\\n1022: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (namestrcmp(&(att->attname), name) == 0)\\n1023: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n1024: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif (rel != NULL)\\n1037: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n1038: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),\\n1039: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"column \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" of relation \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" does not exist\\\",\\n1040: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tname, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));\\n1041: \\t\\t\\t\\telse\\n1042: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n1043: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1047: \\t\\t\\t/* Check for duplicates */\\n1048: \\t\\t\\tif (list_member_int(attnums, attnum))\\n1049: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n1050: #include \\\"utils/rel.h\\\"\\n40: #include \\\"utils/rls.h\\\"\\n41: \\n42: /*\\n43:  *\\t DoCopy executes the SQL COPY statement\\n44:  *\\n45:  * Either unload or reload contents of table <relation>, depending on <from>.\\n46:  * (<from> = true means we are inserting into the table.)  In the \\\"TO\\\" case\\n47:  * we also support copying the output of an arbitrary SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE\\n48:  * or DELETE query.\\n49:  *\\n50:  * If <pipe> is false, transfer is between the table and the file named\\n51:  * <filename>.  Otherwise, transfer is between the table and our regular\\n52:  * input/output stream. The latter could be either stdin/stdout or a\\n53:  * socket, depending on whether we're running under Postmaster control.\\n54:  *\\n55:  * Do not allow a Postgres user without the 'pg_read_server_files' or\\n56:  * 'pg_write_server_files' role to read from or write to a file.\\n57:  *\\n58:  * Do not allow the copy if user doesn't have proper permission to access\\n59:  * the table or the specifically requested columns.\\n60:  */\\n61: void\\n62: DoCopy(ParseState *pstate, const CopyStmt *stmt,\\n63: \\t   int stmt_location, int stmt_len,\\n64: \\t   uint64 *processed)\\n65: {\\n66: \\tbool\\t\\tis_from = stmt->is_from;\\n67: \\tbool\\t\\tpipe = (stmt->filename == NULL);\\n68: \\tRelation\\trel;\\n69: \\tOid\\t\\t\\trelid;\\n70: \\tRawStmt    *query = NULL;\\n71: \\tNode\\t   *whereClause = NULL;\\n72: \\n73: \\t/*\\n74: \\t * Disallow COPY to/from file or program except to users with the\\n75: \\t * appropriate role.\\n76: \\t */\\n77: \\tif (!pipe)\\n78: \\t{\\n79: \\t\\tif (stmt->is_program)\\n80: \\t\\t{\\n81: \\t\\t\\tif (!has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_EXECUTE_SERVER_PROGRAM))\\n82: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n83: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\\n84: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"permission denied to COPY to or from an external program\\\"),\\n85: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" role may COPY to or from an external program.\\\",\\n86: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   \\\"pg_execute_server_program\\\"),\\n87: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \\\"\\n88: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t \\\"psql's \\\\\\\\copy command also works for anyone.\\\")));\\n89: \\t\\t}\\n90: \\t\\telse\\n91: \\t\\t{\\n92: \\t\\t\\tif (is_from && !has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_READ_SERVER_FILES))\\n93: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n94: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"permission denied to COPY from a file\\\"),\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" role may COPY from a file.\\\",\\n97: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   \\\"pg_read_server_files\\\"),\\n98: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \\\"\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t \\\"psql's \\\\\\\\copy command also works for anyone.\\\")));\\n100: \\n101: \\t\\t\\tif (!is_from && !has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_WRITE_SERVER_FILES))\\n102: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n103: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"permission denied to COPY to a file\\\"),\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errdetail(\\\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" role may COPY to a file.\\\",\\n106: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   \\\"pg_write_server_files\\\"),\\n107: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \\\"\\n108: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t \\\"psql's \\\\\\\\copy command also works for anyone.\\\")));\\n109: \\t\\t}\\n110: \\t}\\n111: \\n112: \\tif (stmt->relation)\\n113: \\t{\\n114: \\t\\tLOCKMODE\\tlockmode = is_from ? RowExclusiveLock : AccessShareLock;\\n115: \\t\\tParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;\\n116: \\t\\tRTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;\\n117: \\t\\tTupleDesc\\ttupDesc;\\n118: \\t\\tList\\t   *attnums;\\n119: \\t\\tListCell   *cur;\\n120: \\n121: \\t\\tAssert(!stmt->query);\\n122: \\n123: \\t\\t/* Open and lock the relation, using the appropriate lock type. */\\n124: \\t\\trel = table_openrv(stmt->relation, lockmode);\\n125: \\n126: \\t\\trelid = RelationGetRelid(rel);\\n127: \\n128: \\t\\tnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel, lockmode,\\n129: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   NULL, false, false);\\n130: \\n131: \\t\\tperminfo = nsitem->p_perminfo;\\n132: \\t\\tperminfo->requiredPerms = (is_from ? ACL_INSERT : ACL_SELECT);\\n133: \\n134: \\t\\tif (stmt->whereClause)\\n135: \\t\\t{\\n136: \\t\\t\\tBitmapset  *expr_attrs = NULL;\\n137: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\ti;\\n138: \\n139: \\t\\t\\t/* add nsitem to query namespace */\\n140: \\t\\t\\taddNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);\\n141: \\n142: \\t\\t\\t/* Transform the raw expression tree */\\n143: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = transformExpr(pstate, stmt->whereClause, EXPR_KIND_COPY_WHERE);\\n144: \\n145: \\t\\t\\t/* Make sure it yields a boolean result. */\\n146: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = coerce_to_boolean(pstate, whereClause, \\\"WHERE\\\");\\n147: \\n148: \\t\\t\\t/* we have to fix its collations too */\\n149: \\t\\t\\tassign_expr_collations(pstate, whereClause);\\n150: \\n151: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n152: \\t\\t\\t * Examine all the columns in the WHERE clause expression.  When\\n153: \\t\\t\\t * the whole-row reference is present, examine all the columns of\\n154: \\t\\t\\t * the table.\\n155: \\t\\t\\t */\\n156: \\t\\t\\tpull_varattnos(whereClause, 1, &expr_attrs);\\n157: \\t\\t\\tif (bms_is_member(0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber, expr_attrs))\\n158: \\t\\t\\t{\\n159: \\t\\t\\t\\texpr_attrs = bms_add_range(expr_attrs,\\n160: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   1 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,\\n161: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\\n162: \\t\\t\\t\\texpr_attrs = bms_del_member(expr_attrs, 0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\\n163: \\t\\t\\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t\\ti = -1;\\n166: \\t\\t\\twhile ((i = bms_next_member(expr_attrs, i)) >= 0)\\n167: \\t\\t\\t{\\n168: \\t\\t\\t\\tAttrNumber\\tattno = i + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\\n169: \\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\tAssert(attno != 0);\\n171: \\n172: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n173: \\t\\t\\t\\t * Prohibit generated columns in the WHERE clause.  Stored\\n174: \\t\\t\\t\\t * generated columns are not yet computed when the filtering\\n175: \\t\\t\\t\\t * happens.  Virtual generated columns could probably work (we\\n176: \\t\\t\\t\\t * would need to expand them somewhere around here), but for\\n177: \\t\\t\\t\\t * now we keep them consistent with the stored variant.\\n178: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n179: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (TupleDescAttr(RelationGetDescr(rel), attno - 1)->attgenerated)\\n180: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n181: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),\\n182: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrmsg(\\\"generated columns are not supported in COPY FROM WHERE conditions\\\"),\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terrdetail(\\\"Column \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" is a generated column.\\\",\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t  get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel), attno, false)));\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\n187: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = eval_const_expressions(NULL, whereClause);\\n188: \\n189: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = (Node *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) whereClause, false);\\n190: \\t\\t\\twhereClause = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) whereClause);\\n191: \\t\\t}\\n192: \\n193: \\t\\ttupDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);\\n194: \\t\\tattnums = CopyGetAttnums(tupDesc, rel, stmt->attlist);\\n195: \\t\\tforeach(cur, attnums)\\n196: \\t\\t{\\n197: \\t\\t\\tint\\t\\t\\tattno;\\n198: \\t\\t\\tBitmapset **bms;\\n199: \\n200: \\t\\t\\tattno = lfirst_int(cur) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\\n201: \\t\\t\\tbms = is_from ? &perminfo->insertedCols : &perminfo->selectedCols;\\n202: \\n203: \\t\\t\\t*bms = bms_add_member(*bms, attno);\\n204: \\t\\t}\\n205: \\t\\tExecCheckPermissions(pstate->p_rtable, list_make1(perminfo), true);\\n206: \\n207: \\t\\t/*\\n208: \\t\\t * Permission check for row security policies.\\n209: \\t\\t *\\n210: \\t\\t * check_enable_rls will ereport(ERROR) if the user has requested\\n211: \\t\\t * something invalid and will otherwise indicate if we should enable\\n212: \\t\\t * RLS (returns RLS_ENABLED) or not for this COPY statement.\\n213: \\t\\t *\\n214: \\t\\t * If the relation has a row security policy and we are to apply it\\n215: \\t\\t * then perform a \\\"query\\\" copy and allow the normal query processing\\n216: \\t\\t * to handle the policies.\\n217: \\t\\t *\\n218: \\t\\t * If RLS is not enabled for this, then just fall through to the\\n219: \\t\\t * normal non-filtering relation handling.\\n220: \\t\\t */\\n221: \\t\\tif (check_enable_rls(relid, InvalidOid, false) == RLS_ENABLED)\\n222: \\t\\t{\\n223: \\t\\t\\tSelectStmt *select;\\n224: \\t\\t\\tColumnRef  *cr;\\n225: \\t\\t\\tResTarget  *target;\\n226: \\t\\t\\tRangeVar   *from;\\n227: \\t\\t\\tList\\t   *targetList = NIL;\\n228: \\n229: \\t\\t\\tif (is_from)\\n230: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n231: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY FROM not supported with row-level security\\\"),\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errhint(\\\"Use INSERT statements instead.\\\")));\\n234: \\n235: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n236: \\t\\t\\t * Build target list\\n237: \\t\\t\\t *\\n238: \\t\\t\\t * If no columns are specified in the attribute list of the COPY\\n239: \\t\\t\\t * command, then the target list is 'all' columns. Therefore, '*'\\n240: \\t\\t\\t * should be used as the target list for the resulting SELECT\\n241: \\t\\t\\t * statement.\\n242: \\t\\t\\t *\\n243: \\t\\t\\t * In the case that columns are specified in the attribute list,\\n244: \\t\\t\\t * create a ColumnRef and ResTarget for each column and add them\\n245: \\t\\t\\t * to the target list for the resulting SELECT statement.\\n246: \\t\\t\\t */\\n247: \\t\\t\\tif (!stmt->attlist)\\n248: \\t\\t\\t{\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\tcr->location = -1;\\n252: \\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\\n254: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->name = NULL;\\n255: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\\n256: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\\n257: \\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->location = -1;\\n258: \\n259: \\t\\t\\t\\ttargetList = list_make1(target);\\n260: \\t\\t\\t}\\n261: \\t\\t\\telse\\n262: \\t\\t\\t{\\n263: \\t\\t\\t\\tListCell   *lc;\\n264: \\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\tforeach(lc, stmt->attlist)\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * Build the ColumnRef for each column.  The ColumnRef\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * 'fields' property is a String node that corresponds to\\n270: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t * the column name respectively.\\n271: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n272: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\\n273: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr->fields = list_make1(lfirst(lc));\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcr->location = -1;\\n275: \\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* Build the ResTarget and add the ColumnRef to it. */\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\\n278: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->name = NULL;\\n279: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\\n280: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\\n281: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttarget->location = -1;\\n282: \\n283: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* Add each column to the SELECT statement's target list */\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttargetList = lappend(targetList, target);\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n286: \\t\\t\\t}\\n287: \\n288: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n289: \\t\\t\\t * Build RangeVar for from clause, fully qualified based on the\\n290: \\t\\t\\t * relation which we have opened and locked.  Use \\\"ONLY\\\" so that\\n291: \\t\\t\\t * COPY retrieves rows from only the target table not any\\n292: \\t\\t\\t * inheritance children, the same as when RLS doesn't apply.\\n293: \\t\\t\\t */\\n294: \\t\\t\\tfrom = makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(rel)),\\n295: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(rel)),\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t-1);\\n297: \\t\\t\\tfrom->inh = false;\\t/* apply ONLY */\\n298: \\n299: \\t\\t\\t/* Build query */\\n300: \\t\\t\\tselect = makeNode(SelectStmt);\\n301: \\t\\t\\tselect->targetList = targetList;\\n302: \\t\\t\\tselect->fromClause = list_make1(from);\\n303: \\n304: \\t\\t\\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\\n305: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt = (Node *) select;\\n306: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt_location = stmt_location;\\n307: \\t\\t\\tquery->stmt_len = stmt_len;\\n308: \\n309: \\t\\t\\t/*\\n310: \\t\\t\\t * Close the relation for now, but keep the lock on it to prevent\\n311: \\t\\t\\t * changes between now and when we start the query-based COPY.\\n312: \\t\\t\\t *\\n313: \\t\\t\\t * We'll reopen it later as part of the query-based COPY.\\n314: \\t\\t\\t */\\n315: \\t\\t\\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\\n316: \\t\\t\\trel = NULL;\\n317: \\t\\t}\\n318: \\t}\\n319: \\telse\\n320: \\t{\\n321: \\t\\tAssert(stmt->query);\\n322: \\n323: \\t\\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\\n324: \\t\\tquery->stmt = stmt->query;\\n325: \\t\\tquery->stmt_location = stmt_location;\\n326: \\t\\tquery->stmt_len = stmt_len;\\n327: \\n328: \\t\\trelid = InvalidOid;\\n329: \\t\\trel = NULL;\\n330: \\t}\\n331: \\n332: \\tif (is_from)\\n333: \\t{\\n334: \\t\\tCopyFromState cstate;\\n335: \\n336: \\t\\tAssert(rel);\\n337: \\n338: \\t\\t/* check read-only transaction and parallel mode */\\n339: \\t\\tif (XactReadOnly && !rel->rd_islocaltemp)\\n340: \\t\\t\\tPreventCommandIfReadOnly(\\\"COPY FROM\\\");\\n341: \\n342: \\t\\tcstate = BeginCopyFrom(pstate, rel, whereClause,\\n343: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   stmt->filename, stmt->is_program,\\n344: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t   NULL, stmt->attlist, stmt->options);\\n345: \\t\\t*processed = CopyFrom(cstate);\\t/* copy from file to database */\\n346: \\t\\tEndCopyFrom(cstate);\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\telse\\n349: \\t{\\n350: \\t\\tCopyToState cstate;\\n351: \\n352: \\t\\tcstate = BeginCopyTo(pstate, rel, query, relid,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t stmt->filename, stmt->is_program,\\n354: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t NULL, stmt->attlist, stmt->options);\\n355: \\t\\t*processed = DoCopyTo(cstate);\\t/* copy from database to file */\\n356: \\t\\tEndCopyTo(cstate);\\n357: \\t}\\n358: \\n359: \\tif (rel != NULL)\\n360: \\t\\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: /*\\n364:  * Extract a CopyHeaderChoice value from a DefElem.  This is like\\n365:  * defGetBoolean() but also accepts the special value \\\"match\\\".\\n366:  */\\n367: static CopyHeaderChoice\\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\\n369: {\\n370: \\t/*\\n371: \\t * If no parameter value given, assume \\\"true\\\" is meant.\\n372: \\t */\\n373: \\tif (def->arg == NULL)\\n374: \\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n375: \\n376: \\t/*\\n377: \\t * Allow 0, 1, \\\"true\\\", \\\"false\\\", \\\"on\\\", \\\"off\\\", or \\\"match\\\".\\n378: \\t */\\n379: \\tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\\n380: \\t{\\n381: \\t\\tcase T_Integer:\\n382: \\t\\t\\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\\n383: \\t\\t\\t{\\n384: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n385: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n388: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n389: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t/* otherwise, error out below */\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n391: \\t\\t\\t}\\n392: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n393: \\t\\tdefault:\\n394: \\t\\t\\t{\\n395: \\t\\t\\t\\tchar\\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\\n396: \\n397: \\t\\t\\t\\t/*\\n398: \\t\\t\\t\\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\t */\\n401: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"true\\\") == 0)\\n402: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n403: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"false\\\") == 0)\\n404: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n405: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"on\\\") == 0)\\n406: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"off\\\") == 0)\\n408: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\n409: \\t\\t\\t\\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"match\\\") == 0)\\n410: \\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n411: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif (!is_from)\\n412: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n413: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\\n414: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"cannot use \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" with HEADER in COPY TO\\\",\\n415: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsval)));\\n416: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn COPY_HEADER_MATCH;\\n417: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n418: \\t\\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\t\\tbreak;\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tereport(ERROR,\\n422: \\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\\n423: \\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"%s requires a Boolean value or \\\\\\\"match\\\\\\\"\\\",\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdef->defname)));\\n425: \\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\\t/* keep compiler quiet */\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: /*\\n429:  * Extract a CopyOnErrorChoice value from a DefElem.\\n430:  */\\n431: static CopyOnErrorChoice\\n432: defGetCopyOnErrorChoice(DefElem *def, ParseState *pstate, bool is_from)\\n433: {\\n434: \\tchar\\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\\n435: \\n436: \\tif (!is_from)\\n437: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n438: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\\n439: \\t\\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\\n443: \\n444: \\t/*\\n445: \\t * Allow \\\"stop\\\", or \\\"ignore\\\" values.\\n446: \\t */\\n447: \\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \\\"stop\\\") == 0)\\n448: \\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\\n454: \\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\\n455: \\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"COPY %s \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\" not recognized\\\", \\\"ON_ERROR\\\", sval),\\n456: \\t\\t\\t parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\\n457: \\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP;\\t/* keep compiler quiet */\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: /*\\n461:  * Extract REJECT_LIMIT value from a DefElem.\\n462:  *\\n463:  * REJECT_LIMIT can be specified in two ways: as an int64 for the COPY command\\n464:  * option or as a single-quoted string for the foreign table option using\\n465:  * file_fdw. Therefore this function needs to handle both formats.\\n466:  */\\n467: static int64\\n468: defGetCopyRejectLimitOption(DefElem *def)\\n469: {\\n470: \\tint64\\t\\treject_limit;\\n471: \\n472: \\tif (def->arg == NULL)\\n473: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n474: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\\n475: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"%s requires a numeric value\\\",\\n476: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdef->defname)));\\n477: \\telse if (nodeTag(def->arg) == T_String)\\n478: \\t\\treject_limit = pg_strtoint64(strVal(def->arg));\\n479: \\telse\\n480: \\t\\treject_limit = defGetInt64(def);\\n481: \\n482: \\tif (reject_limit <= 0)\\n483: \\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n484: \\t\\t\\t\\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\\n485: \\t\\t\\t\\t errmsg(\\\"REJECT_LIMIT (%\\\" PRId64 \\\") must be greater than zero\\\",\\n486: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treject_limit)));\\n487: \\n488: \\treturn reject_limit;\\n489: }\\n490: \\n491: /*\\n492:  * Extract a CopyLogVerbosityChoice value from a DefElem.\\n493:  */\\n494: static CopyLogVerbosityChoice\\n495: defGetCopyLogVerbosityChoice(DefElem *def, ParseState *pstate)\\n496: {\\n497: \\tchar\\t   *sval;\\n498: \\n499: \\t/*\\n500: COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_DEFAULT;\\t/* keep compiler quiet */\\n516: }\\n517: \\n518: /*\\n519:  * Process the statement option list for COPY.\\n520:  *\\n521:  * Scan the options list (a list of DefElem) *\\n526:  * This is exported so that external users of the COPY API can sanity-check\\n527:  * a list of options.  In that usage, 'opts_out' can be passed as NULL and\\n528:  * the collected data is just leaked until CurrentMemoryContext is reset.\\n529:  *\\n530:  * Note that additional checking, such as whether column names listed in FORCE\\n531:  * QUOTE actually exist, has to be applied later.  This just checks for\\n532:  * self-consistency of the options list.\\n533:  */\\n534: void\\n535: ProcessCopyOptions(ParseState *pstate,\\n536: \\t\\t\\t\\t   CopyFormatOptions *opts_out,\\n537: \\t\\t\\t\\t   bool is_from,\\n538: \\t\\t\\t\\t   List *options)\\n539: {\\n540: \\tbool\\t\\tformat_specified = false;\\n541: \\tbool\\t\\tfreeze_specified = false;\\n542: \\tbool\\t\\theader_specified = false;\\n543: \\tbool\\t\\ton_error_specified = false;\\n544: \\tbool\\t\\tlog_verbosity_specified = false;\\n545: \\tbool\\t\\treject_limit_specified = false;\\n546: \\tListCell   *option;\\n547: \\n548: \\t/* Support external use for option sanity checking */\\n549: \\tif (opts_out == NULL)\\n550: \\t\\topts_out = (CopyFormatOptions *) palloc0(sizeof(CopyFormatOptions));\\n551: \\n552: \\topts_out->file_encoding = -1;\\n553: \\n554: \\t/* Extract options from the statement node tree */\\n555: \\tforeach(option, options)\\n556: \\t{\\n557: \\t\\tDefElem    *defel = lfirst_node(DefElem, option);\\n558: \\n559: \\t\\tif (strcmp(defel->defname, \\\"format\\\") == 0)\\n560: \\t\\t{\\n561: \\t\\t\\tchar\\t   *fmt = \\t\\t\\t\\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\\n582: \\t\\t\\tfreeze_specified = true;\\n583: \\t\\t\\topts_out->freeze = defGetBoolean(defel);\\n584: \\t\\t}\\n585: \\t\\telse if \\n969: /*\\n970:  * CopyGetAttnums - build an integer list of attnums to be copied\\n971:  *\\n972:  * The input attnamelist is either the user-specified column list,\\n973:  * or NIL if there was none (in which case we want all the non-dropped\\n974:  * columns).\\n975:  *\\n976:  * We don't include generated columns in the generated full list and we don't\\n977:  * allow them to be specified explicitly.  They don't make sense for COPY\\n978:  * FROM, but we could possibly allow them for COPY TO.  But this way it's at\\n979:  * least ensured that whatever we copy out can be copied back in.\\n980:  *\\n981:  * rel can be NULL ... it's only used for error reports.\\n982:  */\\n983: List *\\n984: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1047: \\t\\t\\t/* Check for duplicates */\\n1048: \\t\\t\\tif (list_member_int(attnums, attnum))\\n1049: \\t\\t\\t\\tereport(ERROR,\\n1050: \"utils/acl.h\"\n37: #include \"utils/builtins.h\"\n38: #include \"utils/lsyscache.h\"\n39: #include \"utils/rel.h\"\n40: #include \"utils/rls.h\"\n41: \n42: /*\n43:  *\t DoCopy executes the SQL COPY statement\n44:  *\n45:  * Either unload or reload contents of table <relation>, depending on <from>.\n46:  * (<from> = true means we are inserting into the table.)  In the \"TO\" case\n47:  * we also support copying the output of an arbitrary SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE\n48:  * or DELETE query.\n49:  *\n50:  * If <pipe> is false, transfer is between the table and the file named\n51:  * <filename>.  Otherwise, transfer is between the table and our regular\n52:  * input/output stream. The latter could be either stdin/stdout or a\n53:  * socket, depending on whether we're running under Postmaster control.\n54:  *\n55:  * Do not allow a Postgres user without the 'pg_read_server_files' or\n56:  * 'pg_write_server_files' role to read from or write to a file.\n57:  *\n58:  * Do not allow the copy if user doesn't have proper permission to access\n59:  * the table or the specifically requested columns.\n60:  */\n61: void\n62: DoCopy(ParseState *pstate, const CopyStmt *stmt,\n63: \t   int stmt_location, int stmt_len,\n64: \t   uint64 *processed)\n65: {\n66: \tbool\t\tis_from = stmt->is_from;\n67: \tbool\t\tpipe = (stmt->filename == NULL);\n68: \tRelation\trel;\n69: \tOid\t\t\trelid;\n70: \tRawStmt    *query = NULL;\n71: \tNode\t   *whereClause = NULL;\n72: \n73: \t/*\n74: \t * Disallow COPY to/from file or program except to users with the\n75: \t * appropriate role.\n76: \t */\n77: \tif (!pipe)\n78: \t{\n79: \t\tif (stmt->is_program)\n80: \t\t{\n81: \t\t\tif (!has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_EXECUTE_SERVER_PROGRAM))\n82: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n83: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\n84: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"permission denied to COPY to or from an external program\"),\n85: \t\t\t\t\t\t errdetail(\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\"%s\\\" role may COPY to or from an external program.\",\n86: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"pg_execute_server_program\"),\n87: \t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \"\n88: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t \"psql's \\\\copy command also works for anyone.\")));\n89: \t\t}\n90: \t\telse\n91: \t\t{\n92: \t\t\tif (is_from && !has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_READ_SERVER_FILES))\n93: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n94: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\n95: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"permission denied to COPY from a file\"),\n96: \t\t\t\t\t\t errdetail(\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\"%s\\\" role may COPY from a file.\",\n97: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"pg_read_server_files\"),\n98: \t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \"\n99: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t \"psql's \\\\copy command also works for anyone.\")));\n100: \n101: \t\t\tif (!is_from && !has_privs_of_role(GetUserId(), ROLE_PG_WRITE_SERVER_FILES))\n102: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n103: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),\n104: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"permission denied to COPY to a file\"),\n105: \t\t\t\t\t\t errdetail(\"Only roles with privileges of the \\\"%s\\\" role may COPY to a file.\",\n106: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"pg_write_server_files\"),\n107: \t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Anyone can COPY to stdout or from stdin. \"\n108: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t \"psql's \\\\copy command also works for anyone.\")));\n109: \t\t}\n110: \t}\n111: \n112: \tif (stmt->relation)\n113: \t{\n114: \t\tLOCKMODE\tlockmode = is_from ? RowExclusiveLock : AccessShareLock;\n115: \t\tParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;\n116: \t\tRTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;\n117: \t\tTupleDesc\ttupDesc;\n118: \t\tList\t   *attnums;\n119: \t\tListCell   *cur;\n120: \n121: \t\tAssert(!stmt->query);\n122: \n123: \t\t/* Open and lock the relation, using the appropriate lock type. */\n124: \t\trel = table_openrv(stmt->relation, lockmode);\n125: \n126: \t\trelid = RelationGetRelid(rel);\n127: \n128: \t\tnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel, lockmode,\n129: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   NULL, false, false);\n130: \n131: \t\tperminfo = nsitem->p_perminfo;\n132: \t\tperminfo->requiredPerms = (is_from ? ACL_INSERT : ACL_SELECT);\n133: \n134: \t\tif (stmt->whereClause)\n135: \t\t{\n136: \t\t\tBitmapset  *expr_attrs = NULL;\n137: \t\t\tint\t\t\ti;\n138: \n139: \t\t\t/* add nsitem to query namespace */\n140: \t\t\taddNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);\n141: \n142: \t\t\t/* Transform the raw expression tree */\n143: \t\t\twhereClause = transformExpr(pstate, stmt->whereClause, EXPR_KIND_COPY_WHERE);\n144: \n145: \t\t\t/* Make sure it yields a boolean result. */\n146: \t\t\twhereClause = coerce_to_boolean(pstate, whereClause, \"WHERE\");\n147: \n148: \t\t\t/* we have to fix its collations too */\n149: \t\t\tassign_expr_collations(pstate, whereClause);\n150: \n151: \t\t\t/*\n152: \t\t\t * Examine all the columns in the WHERE clause expression.  When\n153: \t\t\t * the whole-row reference is present, examine all the columns of\n154: \t\t\t * the table.\n155: \t\t\t */\n156: \t\t\tpull_varattnos(whereClause, 1, &expr_attrs);\n157: \t\t\tif (bms_is_member(0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber, expr_attrs))\n158: \t\t\t{\n159: \t\t\t\texpr_attrs = bms_add_range(expr_attrs,\n160: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   1 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,\n161: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\n162: \t\t\t\texpr_attrs = bms_del_member(expr_attrs, 0 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);\n163: \t\t\t}\n164: \n165: \t\t\ti = -1;\n166: \t\t\twhile ((i = bms_next_member(expr_attrs, i)) >= 0)\n167: \t\t\t{\n168: \t\t\t\tAttrNumber\tattno = i + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\n169: \n170: \t\t\t\tAssert(attno != 0);\n171: \n172: \t\t\t\t/*\n173: \t\t\t\t * Prohibit generated columns in the WHERE clause.  Stored\n174: \t\t\t\t * generated columns are not yet computed when the filtering\n175: \t\t\t\t * happens.  Virtual generated columns could probably work (we\n176: \t\t\t\t * would need to expand them somewhere around here), but for\n177: \t\t\t\t * now we keep them consistent with the stored variant.\n178: \t\t\t\t */\n179: \t\t\t\tif (TupleDescAttr(RelationGetDescr(rel), attno - 1)->attgenerated)\n180: \t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n181: \t\t\t\t\t\t\terrcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),\n182: \t\t\t\t\t\t\terrmsg(\"generated columns are not supported in COPY FROM WHERE conditions\"),\n183: \t\t\t\t\t\t\terrdetail(\"Column \\\"%s\\\" is a generated column.\",\n184: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  get_attname(RelationGetRelid(rel), attno, false)));\n185: \t\t\t}\n186: \n187: \t\t\twhereClause = eval_const_expressions(NULL, whereClause);\n188: \n189: \t\t\twhereClause = (Node *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) whereClause, false);\n190: \t\t\twhereClause = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) whereClause);\n191: \t\t}\n192: \n193: \t\ttupDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);\n194: \t\tattnums = CopyGetAttnums(tupDesc, rel, stmt->attlist);\n195: \t\tforeach(cur, attnums)\n196: \t\t{\n197: \t\t\tint\t\t\tattno;\n198: \t\t\tBitmapset **bms;\n199: \n200: \t\t\tattno = lfirst_int(cur) - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;\n201: \t\t\tbms = is_from ? &perminfo->insertedCols : &perminfo->selectedCols;\n202: \n203: \t\t\t*bms = bms_add_member(*bms, attno);\n204: \t\t}\n205: \t\tExecCheckPermissions(pstate->p_rtable, list_make1(perminfo), true);\n206: \n207: \t\t/*\n208: \t\t * Permission check for row security policies.\n209: \t\t *\n210: \t\t * check_enable_rls will ereport(ERROR) if the user has requested\n211: \t\t * something invalid and will otherwise indicate if we should enable\n212: \t\t * RLS (returns RLS_ENABLED) or not for this COPY statement.\n213: \t\t *\n214: \t\t * If the relation has a row security policy and we are to apply it\n215: \t\t * then perform a \"query\" copy and allow the normal query processing\n216: \t\t * to handle the policies.\n217: \t\t *\n218: \t\t * If RLS is not enabled for this, then just fall through to the\n219: \t\t * normal non-filtering relation handling.\n220: \t\t */\n221: \t\tif (check_enable_rls(relid, InvalidOid, false) == RLS_ENABLED)\n222: \t\t{\n223: \t\t\tSelectStmt *select;\n224: \t\t\tColumnRef  *cr;\n225: \t\t\tResTarget  *target;\n226: \t\t\tRangeVar   *from;\n227: \t\t\tList\t   *targetList = NIL;\n228: \n229: \t\t\tif (is_from)\n230: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n231: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n232: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY FROM not supported with row-level security\"),\n233: \t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Use INSERT statements instead.\")));\n234: \n235: \t\t\t/*\n236: \t\t\t * Build target list\n237: \t\t\t *\n238: \t\t\t * If no columns are specified in the attribute list of the COPY\n239: \t\t\t * command, then the target list is 'all' columns. Therefore, '*'\n240: \t\t\t * should be used as the target list for the resulting SELECT\n241: \t\t\t * statement.\n242: \t\t\t *\n243: \t\t\t * In the case that columns are specified in the attribute list,\n244: \t\t\t * create a ColumnRef and ResTarget for each column and add them\n245: \t\t\t * to the target list for the resulting SELECT statement.\n246: \t\t\t */\n247: \t\t\tif (!stmt->attlist)\n248: \t\t\t{\n249: \t\t\t\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n250: \t\t\t\tcr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\n251: \t\t\t\tcr->location = -1;\n252: \n253: \t\t\t\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\n254: \t\t\t\ttarget->name = NULL;\n255: \t\t\t\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\n256: \t\t\t\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\n257: \t\t\t\ttarget->location = -1;\n258: \n259: \t\t\t\ttargetList = list_make1(target);\n260: \t\t\t}\n261: \t\t\telse\n262: \t\t\t{\n263: \t\t\t\tListCell   *lc;\n264: \n265: \t\t\t\tforeach(lc, stmt->attlist)\n266: \t\t\t\t{\n267: \t\t\t\t\t/*\n268: \t\t\t\t\t * Build the ColumnRef for each column.  The ColumnRef\n269: \t\t\t\t\t * 'fields' property is a String node that corresponds to\n270: \t\t\t\t\t * the column name respectively.\n271: \t\t\t\t\t */\n272: \t\t\t\t\tcr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n273: \t\t\t\t\tcr->fields = list_make1(lfirst(lc));\n274: \t\t\t\t\tcr->location = -1;\n275: \n276: \t\t\t\t\t/* Build the ResTarget and add the ColumnRef to it. */\n277: \t\t\t\t\ttarget = makeNode(ResTarget);\n278: \t\t\t\t\ttarget->name = NULL;\n279: \t\t\t\t\ttarget->indirection = NIL;\n280: \t\t\t\t\ttarget->val = (Node *) cr;\n281: \t\t\t\t\ttarget->location = -1;\n282: \n283: \t\t\t\t\t/* Add each column to the SELECT statement's target list */\n284: \t\t\t\t\ttargetList = lappend(targetList, target);\n285: \t\t\t\t}\n286: \t\t\t}\n287: \n288: \t\t\t/*\n289: \t\t\t * Build RangeVar for from clause, fully qualified based on the\n290: \t\t\t * relation which we have opened and locked.  Use \"ONLY\" so that\n291: \t\t\t * COPY retrieves rows from only the target table not any\n292: \t\t\t * inheritance children, the same as when RLS doesn't apply.\n293: \t\t\t */\n294: \t\t\tfrom = makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(rel)),\n295: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpstrdup(RelationGetRelationName(rel)),\n296: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t-1);\n297: \t\t\tfrom->inh = false;\t/* apply ONLY */\n298: \n299: \t\t\t/* Build query */\n300: \t\t\tselect = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n301: \t\t\tselect->targetList = targetList;\n302: \t\t\tselect->fromClause = list_make1(from);\n303: \n304: \t\t\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\n305: \t\t\tquery->stmt = (Node *) select;\n306: \t\t\tquery->stmt_location = stmt_location;\n307: \t\t\tquery->stmt_len = stmt_len;\n308: \n309: \t\t\t/*\n310: \t\t\t * Close the relation for now, but keep the lock on it to prevent\n311: \t\t\t * changes between now and when we start the query-based COPY.\n312: \t\t\t *\n313: \t\t\t * We'll reopen it later as part of the query-based COPY.\n314: \t\t\t */\n315: \t\t\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\n316: \t\t\trel = NULL;\n317: \t\t}\n318: \t}\n319: \telse\n320: \t{\n321: \t\tAssert(stmt->query);\n322: \n323: \t\tquery = makeNode(RawStmt);\n324: \t\tquery->stmt = stmt->query;\n325: \t\tquery->stmt_location = stmt_location;\n326: \t\tquery->stmt_len = stmt_len;\n327: \n328: \t\trelid = InvalidOid;\n329: \t\trel = NULL;\n330: \t}\n331: \n332: \tif (is_from)\n333: \t{\n334: \t\tCopyFromState cstate;\n335: \n336: \t\tAssert(rel);\n337: \n338: \t\t/* check read-only transaction and parallel mode */\n339: \t\tif (XactReadOnly && !rel->rd_islocaltemp)\n340: \t\t\tPreventCommandIfReadOnly(\"COPY FROM\");\n341: \n342: \t\tcstate = BeginCopyFrom(pstate, rel, whereClause,\n343: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t   stmt->filename, stmt->is_program,\n344: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t   NULL, stmt->attlist, stmt->options);\n345: \t\t*processed = CopyFrom(cstate);\t/* copy from file to database */\n346: \t\tEndCopyFrom(cstate);\n347: \t}\n348: \telse\n349: \t{\n350: \t\tCopyToState cstate;\n351: \n352: \t\tcstate = BeginCopyTo(pstate, rel, query, relid,\n353: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t stmt->filename, stmt->is_program,\n354: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t NULL, stmt->attlist, stmt->options);\n355: \t\t*processed = DoCopyTo(cstate);\t/* copy from database to file */\n356: \t\tEndCopyTo(cstate);\n357: \t}\n358: \n359: \tif (rel != NULL)\n360: \t\ttable_close(rel, NoLock);\n361: }\n362: \n363: /*\n364:  * Extract a CopyHeaderChoice value from a DefElem.  This is like\n365:  * defGetBoolean() but also accepts the special value \"match\".\n366:  */\n367: static CopyHeaderChoice\n368: defGetCopyHeaderChoice(DefElem *def, bool is_from)\n369: {\n370: \t/*\n371: \t * If no parameter value given, assume \"true\" is meant.\n372: \t */\n373: \tif (def->arg == NULL)\n374: \t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n375: \n376: \t/*\n377: \t * Allow 0, 1, \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", or \"match\".\n378: \t */\n379: \tswitch (nodeTag(def->arg))\n380: \t{\n381: \t\tcase T_Integer:\n382: \t\t\tswitch (intVal(def->arg))\n383: \t\t\t{\n384: \t\t\t\tcase 0:\n385: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n386: \t\t\t\tcase 1:\n387: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n388: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n389: \t\t\t\t\t/* otherwise, error out below */\n390: \t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n391: \t\t\t}\n392: \t\t\tbreak;\n393: \t\tdefault:\n394: \t\t\t{\n395: \t\t\t\tchar\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\n396: \n397: \t\t\t\t/*\n398: \t\t\t\t * The set of strings accepted here should match up with the\n399: \t\t\t\t * grammar's opt_boolean_or_string production.\n400: \t\t\t\t */\n401: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"true\") == 0)\n402: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n403: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"false\") == 0)\n404: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n405: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"on\") == 0)\n406: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_TRUE;\n407: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"off\") == 0)\n408: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\n409: \t\t\t\tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"match\") == 0)\n410: \t\t\t\t{\n411: \t\t\t\t\tif (!is_from)\n412: \t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n413: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n414: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use \\\"%s\\\" with HEADER in COPY TO\",\n415: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tsval)));\n416: \t\t\t\t\treturn COPY_HEADER_MATCH;\n417: \t\t\t\t}\n418: \t\t\t}\n419: \t\t\tbreak;\n420: \t}\n421: \tereport(ERROR,\n422: \t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n423: \t\t\t errmsg(\"%s requires a Boolean value or \\\"match\\\"\",\n424: \t\t\t\t\tdef->defname)));\n425: \treturn COPY_HEADER_FALSE;\t/* keep compiler quiet */\n426: }\n427: \n428: /*\n429:  * Extract a CopyOnErrorChoice value from a DefElem.\n430:  */\n431: static CopyOnErrorChoice\n432: defGetCopyOnErrorChoice(DefElem *def, ParseState *pstate, bool is_from)\n433: {\n434: \tchar\t   *sval = defGetString(def);\n435: \n436: \tif (!is_from)\n437: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n438: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n439: \t\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR,\n440: \t\t second %s is a COPY with direction, e.g. COPY TO */\n441: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s cannot be used with %s\", \"ON_ERROR\", \"COPY TO\"),\n442: \t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\n443: \n444: \t/*\n445: \t * Allow \"stop\", or \"ignore\" values.\n446: \t */\n447: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"stop\") == 0)\n448: \t\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP;\n449: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"ignore\") == 0)\n450: \t\treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_IGNORE;\n451: \n452: \tereport(ERROR,\n453: \t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n454: \t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n455: \t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s \\\"%s\\\" not recognized\", \"ON_ERROR\", sval),\n456: \t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\n457: \treturn COPY_ON_ERROR_STOP;\t/* keep compiler quiet */\n458: }\n459: \n460: /*\n461:  * Extract REJECT_LIMIT value from a DefElem.\n462:  *\n463:  * REJECT_LIMIT can be specified in two ways: as an int64 for the COPY command\n464:  * option or as a single-quoted string for the foreign table option using\n465:  * file_fdw. Therefore this function needs to handle both formats.\n466:  */\n467: static int64\n468: defGetCopyRejectLimitOption(DefElem *def)\n469: {\n470: \tint64\t\treject_limit;\n471: \n472: \tif (def->arg == NULL)\n473: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n474: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n475: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s requires a numeric value\",\n476: \t\t\t\t\t\tdef->defname)));\n477: \telse if (nodeTag(def->arg) == T_String)\n478: \t\treject_limit = pg_strtoint64(strVal(def->arg));\n479: \telse\n480: \t\treject_limit = defGetInt64(def);\n481: \n482: \tif (reject_limit <= 0)\n483: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n484: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n485: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"REJECT_LIMIT (%\" PRId64 \") must be greater than zero\",\n486: \t\t\t\t\t\treject_limit)));\n487: \n488: \treturn reject_limit;\n489: }\n490: \n491: /*\n492:  * Extract a CopyLogVerbosityChoice value from a DefElem.\n493:  */\n494: static CopyLogVerbosityChoice\n495: defGetCopyLogVerbosityChoice(DefElem *def, ParseState *pstate)\n496: {\n497: \tchar\t   *sval;\n498: \n499: \t/*\n500: \t * Allow \"silent\", \"default\", or \"verbose\" values.\n501: \t */\n502: \tsval = defGetString(def);\n503: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"silent\") == 0)\n504: \t\treturn COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_SILENT;\n505: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"default\") == 0)\n506: \t\treturn COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_DEFAULT;\n507: \tif (pg_strcasecmp(sval, \"verbose\") == 0)\n508: \t\treturn COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_VERBOSE;\n509: \n510: \tereport(ERROR,\n511: \t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n512: \t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n513: \t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s \\\"%s\\\" not recognized\", \"LOG_VERBOSITY\", sval),\n514: \t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, def->location)));\n515: \treturn COPY_LOG_VERBOSITY_DEFAULT;\t/* keep compiler quiet */\n516: }\n517: \n518: /*\n519:  * Process the statement option list for COPY.\n520:  *\n521:  * Scan the options list (a list of DefElem) and transpose the information\n522:  * into *opts_out, applying appropriate error checking.\n523:  *\n524:  * If 'opts_out' is not NULL, it is assumed to be filled with zeroes initially.\n525:  *\n526:  * This is exported so that external users of the COPY API can sanity-check\n527:  * a list of options.  In that usage, 'opts_out' can be passed as NULL and\n528:  * the collected data is just leaked until CurrentMemoryContext is reset.\n529:  *\n530:  * Note that additional checking, such as whether column names listed in FORCE\n531:  * QUOTE actually exist, has to be applied later.  This just checks for\n532:  * self-consistency of the options list.\n533:  */\n534: void\n535: ProcessCopyOptions(ParseState *pstate,\n536: \t\t\t\t   CopyFormatOptions *opts_out,\n537: \t\t\t\t   bool is_from,\n538: \t\t\t\t   List *options)\n539: {\n540: \tbool\t\tformat_specified = false;\n541: \tbool\t\tfreeze_specified = false;\n542: \tbool\t\theader_specified = false;\n543: \tbool\t\ton_error_specified = false;\n544: \tbool\t\tlog_verbosity_specified = false;\n545: \tbool\t\treject_limit_specified = false;\n546: \tListCell   *option;\n547: \n548: \t/* Support external use for option sanity checking */\n549: \tif (opts_out == NULL)\n550: \t\topts_out = (CopyFormatOptions *) palloc0(sizeof(CopyFormatOptions));\n551: \n552: \topts_out->file_encoding = -1;\n553: \n554: \t/* Extract options from the statement node tree */\n555: \tforeach(option, options)\n556: \t{\n557: \t\tDefElem    *defel = lfirst_node(DefElem, option);\n558: \n559: \t\tif (strcmp(defel->defname, \"format\") == 0)\n560: \t\t{\n561: \t\t\tchar\t   *fmt = defGetString(defel);\n562: \n563: \t\t\tif (format_specified)\n564: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n565: \t\t\tformat_specified = true;\n566: \t\t\tif (strcmp(fmt, \"text\") == 0)\n567: \t\t\t\t /* default format */ ;\n568: \t\t\telse if (strcmp(fmt, \"csv\") == 0)\n569: \t\t\t\topts_out->csv_mode = true;\n570: \t\t\telse if (strcmp(fmt, \"binary\") == 0)\n571: \t\t\t\topts_out->binary = true;\n572: \t\t\telse\n573: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n574: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n575: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY format \\\"%s\\\" not recognized\", fmt),\n576: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n577: \t\t}\n578: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"freeze\") == 0)\n579: \t\t{\n580: \t\t\tif (freeze_specified)\n581: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n582: \t\t\tfreeze_specified = true;\n583: \t\t\topts_out->freeze = defGetBoolean(defel);\n584: \t\t}\n585: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"delimiter\") == 0)\n586: \t\t{\n587: \t\t\tif (opts_out->delim)\n588: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n589: \t\t\topts_out->delim = defGetString(defel);\n590: \t\t}\n591: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"null\") == 0)\n592: \t\t{\n593: \t\t\tif (opts_out->null_print)\n594: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n595: \t\t\topts_out->null_print = defGetString(defel);\n596: \t\t}\n597: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"default\") == 0)\n598: \t\t{\n599: \t\t\tif (opts_out->default_print)\n600: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n601: \t\t\topts_out->default_print = defGetString(defel);\n602: \t\t}\n603: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"header\") == 0)\n604: \t\t{\n605: \t\t\tif (header_specified)\n606: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n607: \t\t\theader_specified = true;\n608: \t\t\topts_out->header_line = defGetCopyHeaderChoice(defel, is_from);\n609: \t\t}\n610: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"quote\") == 0)\n611: \t\t{\n612: \t\t\tif (opts_out->quote)\n613: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n614: \t\t\topts_out->quote = defGetString(defel);\n615: \t\t}\n616: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"escape\") == 0)\n617: \t\t{\n618: \t\t\tif (opts_out->escape)\n619: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n620: \t\t\topts_out->escape = defGetString(defel);\n621: \t\t}\n622: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"force_quote\") == 0)\n623: \t\t{\n624: \t\t\tif (opts_out->force_quote || opts_out->force_quote_all)\n625: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n626: \t\t\tif (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, A_Star))\n627: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_quote_all = true;\n628: \t\t\telse if (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, List))\n629: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_quote = castNode(List, defel->arg);\n630: \t\t\telse\n631: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n632: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n633: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"argument to option \\\"%s\\\" must be a list of column names\",\n634: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n635: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n636: \t\t}\n637: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"force_not_null\") == 0)\n638: \t\t{\n639: \t\t\tif (opts_out->force_notnull || opts_out->force_notnull_all)\n640: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n641: \t\t\tif (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, A_Star))\n642: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_notnull_all = true;\n643: \t\t\telse if (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, List))\n644: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_notnull = castNode(List, defel->arg);\n645: \t\t\telse\n646: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n647: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n648: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"argument to option \\\"%s\\\" must be a list of column names\",\n649: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n650: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n651: \t\t}\n652: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"force_null\") == 0)\n653: \t\t{\n654: \t\t\tif (opts_out->force_null || opts_out->force_null_all)\n655: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n656: \t\t\tif (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, A_Star))\n657: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_null_all = true;\n658: \t\t\telse if (defel->arg && IsA(defel->arg, List))\n659: \t\t\t\topts_out->force_null = castNode(List, defel->arg);\n660: \t\t\telse\n661: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n662: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n663: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"argument to option \\\"%s\\\" must be a list of column names\",\n664: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n665: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n666: \t\t}\n667: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"convert_selectively\") == 0)\n668: \t\t{\n669: \t\t\t/*\n670: \t\t\t * Undocumented, not-accessible-from-SQL option: convert only the\n671: \t\t\t * named columns to binary form, storing the rest as NULLs. It's\n672: \t\t\t * allowed for the column list to be NIL.\n673: \t\t\t */\n674: \t\t\tif (opts_out->convert_selectively)\n675: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n676: \t\t\topts_out->convert_selectively = true;\n677: \t\t\tif (defel->arg == NULL || IsA(defel->arg, List))\n678: \t\t\t\topts_out->convert_select = castNode(List, defel->arg);\n679: \t\t\telse\n680: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n681: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n682: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"argument to option \\\"%s\\\" must be a list of column names\",\n683: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n684: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n685: \t\t}\n686: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"encoding\") == 0)\n687: \t\t{\n688: \t\t\tif (opts_out->file_encoding >= 0)\n689: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n690: \t\t\topts_out->file_encoding = pg_char_to_encoding(defGetString(defel));\n691: \t\t\tif (opts_out->file_encoding < 0)\n692: \t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n693: \t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n694: \t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"argument to option \\\"%s\\\" must be a valid encoding name\",\n695: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n696: \t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n697: \t\t}\n698: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"on_error\") == 0)\n699: \t\t{\n700: \t\t\tif (on_error_specified)\n701: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n702: \t\t\ton_error_specified = true;\n703: \t\t\topts_out->on_error = defGetCopyOnErrorChoice(defel, pstate, is_from);\n704: \t\t}\n705: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"log_verbosity\") == 0)\n706: \t\t{\n707: \t\t\tif (log_verbosity_specified)\n708: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n709: \t\t\tlog_verbosity_specified = true;\n710: \t\t\topts_out->log_verbosity = defGetCopyLogVerbosityChoice(defel, pstate);\n711: \t\t}\n712: \t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"reject_limit\") == 0)\n713: \t\t{\n714: \t\t\tif (reject_limit_specified)\n715: \t\t\t\terrorConflictingDefElem(defel, pstate);\n716: \t\t\treject_limit_specified = true;\n717: \t\t\topts_out->reject_limit = defGetCopyRejectLimitOption(defel);\n718: \t\t}\n719: \t\telse\n720: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n721: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n722: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"option \\\"%s\\\" not recognized\",\n723: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n724: \t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(pstate, defel->location)));\n725: \t}\n726: \n727: \t/*\n728: \t * Check for incompatible options (must do these three before inserting\n729: \t * defaults)\n730: \t */\n731: \tif (opts_out->binary && opts_out->delim)\n732: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n733: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n734: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n735: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot specify %s in BINARY mode\", \"DELIMITER\")));\n736: \n737: \tif (opts_out->binary && opts_out->null_print)\n738: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n739: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n740: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot specify %s in BINARY mode\", \"NULL\")));\n741: \n742: \tif (opts_out->binary && opts_out->default_print)\n743: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n744: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n745: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot specify %s in BINARY mode\", \"DEFAULT\")));\n746: \n747: \t/* Set defaults for omitted options */\n748: \tif (!opts_out->delim)\n749: \t\topts_out->delim = opts_out->csv_mode ? \",\" : \"\\t\";\n750: \n751: \tif (!opts_out->null_print)\n752: \t\topts_out->null_print = opts_out->csv_mode ? \"\" : \"\\\\N\";\n753: \topts_out->null_print_len = strlen(opts_out->null_print);\n754: \n755: \tif (opts_out->csv_mode)\n756: \t{\n757: \t\tif (!opts_out->quote)\n758: \t\t\topts_out->quote = \"\\\"\";\n759: \t\tif (!opts_out->escape)\n760: \t\t\topts_out->escape = opts_out->quote;\n761: \t}\n762: \n763: \t/* Only single-byte delimiter strings are supported. */\n764: \tif (strlen(opts_out->delim) != 1)\n765: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n766: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n767: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY delimiter must be a single one-byte character\")));\n768: \n769: \t/* Disallow end-of-line characters */\n770: \tif (strchr(opts_out->delim, '\\r') != NULL ||\n771: \t\tstrchr(opts_out->delim, '\\n') != NULL)\n772: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n773: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n774: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY delimiter cannot be newline or carriage return\")));\n775: \n776: \tif (strchr(opts_out->null_print, '\\r') != NULL ||\n777: \t\tstrchr(opts_out->null_print, '\\n') != NULL)\n778: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n779: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n780: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY null representation cannot use newline or carriage return\")));\n781: \n782: \tif (opts_out->default_print)\n783: \t{\n784: \t\topts_out->default_print_len = strlen(opts_out->default_print);\n785: \n786: \t\tif (strchr(opts_out->default_print, '\\r') != NULL ||\n787: \t\t\tstrchr(opts_out->default_print, '\\n') != NULL)\n788: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n789: \t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n790: \t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY default representation cannot use newline or carriage return\")));\n791: \t}\n792: \n793: \t/*\n794: \t * Disallow unsafe delimiter characters in non-CSV mode.  We can't allow\n795: \t * backslash because it would be ambiguous.  We can't allow the other\n796: \t * cases because data characters matching the delimiter must be\n797: \t * backslashed, and certain backslash combinations are interpreted\n798: \t * non-literally by COPY IN.  Disallowing all lower case ASCII letters is\n799: \t * more than strictly necessary, but seems best for consistency and\n800: \t * future-proofing.  Likewise we disallow all digits though only octal\n801: \t * digits are actually dangerous.\n802: \t */\n803: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode &&\n804: \t\tstrchr(\"\\\\.abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789\",\n805: \t\t\t   opts_out->delim[0]) != NULL)\n806: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n807: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n808: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY delimiter cannot be \\\"%s\\\"\", opts_out->delim)));\n809: \n810: \t/* Check header */\n811: \tif (opts_out->binary && opts_out->header_line)\n812: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n813: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n814: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n815: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot specify %s in BINARY mode\", \"HEADER\")));\n816: \n817: \t/* Check quote */\n818: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode && opts_out->quote != NULL)\n819: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n820: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n821: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n822: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s requires CSV mode\", \"QUOTE\")));\n823: \n824: \tif (opts_out->csv_mode && strlen(opts_out->quote) != 1)\n825: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n826: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n827: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY quote must be a single one-byte character\")));\n828: \n829: \tif (opts_out->csv_mode && opts_out->delim[0] == opts_out->quote[0])\n830: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n831: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n832: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY delimiter and quote must be different\")));\n833: \n834: \t/* Check escape */\n835: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode && opts_out->escape != NULL)\n836: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n837: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n838: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n839: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s requires CSV mode\", \"ESCAPE\")));\n840: \n841: \tif (opts_out->csv_mode && strlen(opts_out->escape) != 1)\n842: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n843: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n844: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY escape must be a single one-byte character\")));\n845: \n846: \t/* Check force_quote */\n847: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode && (opts_out->force_quote || opts_out->force_quote_all))\n848: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n849: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n850: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n851: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s requires CSV mode\", \"FORCE_QUOTE\")));\n852: \tif ((opts_out->force_quote || opts_out->force_quote_all) && is_from)\n853: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n854: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n855: \t\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR,\n856: \t\t second %s is a COPY with direction, e.g. COPY TO */\n857: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s cannot be used with %s\", \"FORCE_QUOTE\",\n858: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"COPY FROM\")));\n859: \n860: \t/* Check force_notnull */\n861: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode && (opts_out->force_notnull != NIL ||\n862: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\topts_out->force_notnull_all))\n863: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n864: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n865: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n866: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s requires CSV mode\", \"FORCE_NOT_NULL\")));\n867: \tif ((opts_out->force_notnull != NIL || opts_out->force_notnull_all) &&\n868: \t\t!is_from)\n869: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n870: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n871: \t\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR,\n872: \t\t second %s is a COPY with direction, e.g. COPY TO */\n873: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s cannot be used with %s\", \"FORCE_NOT_NULL\",\n874: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"COPY TO\")));\n875: \n876: \t/* Check force_null */\n877: \tif (!opts_out->csv_mode && (opts_out->force_null != NIL ||\n878: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\topts_out->force_null_all))\n879: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n880: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n881: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR */\n882: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s requires CSV mode\", \"FORCE_NULL\")));\n883: \n884: \tif ((opts_out->force_null != NIL || opts_out->force_null_all) &&\n885: \t\t!is_from)\n886: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n887: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n888: \t\t/*- translator: first %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. ON_ERROR,\n889: \t\t second %s is a COPY with direction, e.g. COPY TO */\n890: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY %s cannot be used with %s\", \"FORCE_NULL\",\n891: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"COPY TO\")));\n892: \n893: \t/* Don't allow the delimiter to appear in the null string. */\n894: \tif (strchr(opts_out->null_print, opts_out->delim[0]) != NULL)\n895: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n896: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n897: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. NULL */\n898: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"COPY delimiter character must not appear in the %s specification\",\n899: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"NULL\")));\n900: \n901: \t/* Don't allow the CSV quote char to appear in the null string. */\n902: \tif (opts_out->csv_mode &&\n903: \t\tstrchr(opts_out->null_print, opts_out->quote[0]) != NULL)\n904: \t\tereport(ERROR,\n905: \t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n906: \t\t/*- translator: %s is the name of a COPY option, e.g. NULL */\n907: \t\t\t\t errmsg(\"CSV quote character must not appear in the %s specification\",\n908: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"NULL\")));\n909: \n910: \t/* Check freeze */\n911: \tif TO\")));\n928: \n929: \t\t/* Don't allow the delimiter to appear in the default string. */\n930: \t\tif (strchr(opts_out->default_print, opts_out->delim[0]) != NULL)\n931: \t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n932: want all the non-dropped\n974:  * columns).\n975:  *\n976:  * We don't include generated columns in the generated full list and we don't\n977:  * allow them to be specified explicitly.  They don't make sense for COPY\n978:  * FROM, but we could possibly allow them for COPY TO.  But this way it's at\n979:  * least ensured that whatever we copy out can be copied back in.\n980:  *\n981:  * rel can be NULL ... it's only used for error reports.\n982:  */\n983: List *\n984: CopyGetAttnums(TupleDesc tupDesc, Relation rel, List *attnamelist)\n985: {\n986: \tList\t   *attnums = NIL;\n987: \n988: \tif (attnamelist == NIL)\n989: \t{\n990: \t\t/* Generate default column list */\n991: \t\tint\t\t\tattr_count = tupDesc->natts;\n992: \t\tint\t\t\ti;\n993: \n994: \t\tfor (i = 0; i < attr_count; i++)\n995: \t\t{\n996: \t\t\tCompactAttribute *attr = TupleDescCompactAttr(tupDesc, i);\n997: \n998: \t\t\tif (attr->attisdropped || attr->attgenerated)\n999: \t\t\t\tcontinue;\n1000: \t\t\tattnums = lappend_int(attnums, i + 1);\n1001: \t\t}\n1002: \t}\n1003: \telse\n1004: \t{\n1005: \t\t/* Validate the user-supplied list and extract attnums */\n1006: \t\tListCell   *l;\n1007: \n1008: \t\tforeach(l, attnamelist)\n1009: \t\t{\n1010: \t\t\tchar\t   *name = strVal(lfirst(l));\n1011: \t\t\tint\t\t\tattnum;\n1012: \t\t\tint\t\t\ti;\n1013: \n1014: \t\t\t/* Lookup column name */\n1015: \t\t\tattnum = InvalidAttrNumber;\n1016: \t\t\tfor (i = 0;
